Now before I close this module, I want to leave you with one more very important concept. It's one that I know you can relate to in your life. Let me just explain this with you up front here. There are only two paths in life for a person to take. There's the path of reasons, and I call these reason people. These are people of all the reasons about why they can't get what they want in life, why they can't hit their sales quarter, why the leads suck, why everything is wrong. It's reason people. That's one path, the path of reasons, and the second path, which is the path of results. These are people who find a way no matter what. You have reason people who are literally disempowered, and results people who live these empowered, fruitful lives at the highest level, they burn bright. These are the leaders, the mooks and shakers of society. You know, I have this story. I call them ducks and eagles. I look at these ducks as these low-level reason people that spend their whole life quacking around and it's like smelly duck pond filled with duck, excuse me, my expression here. These are ducks live life at the lowest level, and then you have eagles who are the people that actually find a way. They soar. You have ducks, and you have eagles. The hardest thing in the world to do is to turn a duck into an eagle. That's a very, very difficult thing to do. Again, I've done it in the past. It's very hard. It takes like massive work with beliefs and values and all this sort of stuff. Here's the beauty. If you're taking this course right now as you are, you can't possibly be a duck because ducks never sign up for courses like this. They will be like, oh my God, no, no, improvement, no. Why? Because it goes against their reason. They have a reason why they're not succeeding. It has nothing to do with them not knowing something. It's everyone else's fault. They're blaming. They're justifying. You get it? Reasons versus results, ducks versus eagles. Again, I want you to nail this ducks eagles concept of your psychology here because if you ever see yourself in the moment taking on a duck-like mentality which starts with this impossibility notion of what can't be done, you've got to change it. You immediately get a snap at it. There are ducks everywhere, and I despise them. You can sniff them out a mile away because they spend 90% of their day wallowing around in this giant duck pond, feeling all your smelly ducks quacking away with that reason after reason after reason of why they can't get what they want like, why they can't get things done. That's what they spend their lives doing, and you can't influence when you're a duck. Eagles are the ones who influence. Eagles find a way. And just so you know, as I said before, the most difficult thing in the world to do is to turn a duck into an eagle, which is an ancillary benefit, by the way, of you taking that behavioral test in the beginning of this training. It's able to weed out any of the ducks that might have slipped through the cracks here, right? Disguise themselves as eagles, you know, as a sort of business trying to hide your wet feet and your ridiculous bill from a true eagle. Anyway, the truth is, I don't need to look at that test to know that you're not a duck. You wouldn't be here right now if you were. You'd have run the other way or actually waddled the other way once you realized the straight line was the real deal and could make you rich. Why? Because it's anathema to a duck. So, it'll quack, quack, quack, a thousand different reasons why it won't work for him. And again, that's the story here. You notice the ducks around you. They're everywhere. I run into them personally all the time, all over the world. Here's an example of a typical duck encounter, Big Bear Mountain. July 4th, it's a mountain in California, you go skiing, right? It's a big holiday weekend in the United States. It's not the nicest place in the world, but it's, you know, a decent place, somewhat near where I live in Southern California. It's up in the mountains. Anyway, so I rented this very nice cabin, and a nice resort up there in Big Bear. I wanted to take my two kids and rent jet skis. And I'd go down to the front lobby to rent these jet skis, and you could see them outside there right there, these four jet skis. They're in the dock, right, through the window behind this lady at the counter, right? It's about 12, 20 in the afternoon. And I say to this woman behind the desk, she's a young girl, 22 years old, I say, you know, excuse me, can I, can I please rent those jet skis? And she says, oh, I'm sorry, they're rented. And I'm like, really? I'm like, but they're right there behind you. She says, oh, no, no, no, someone's already rented them. I said, for the entire hour, and she goes, yeah, yeah, they're rented for the whole hour. So I said, well, I guess they didn't show up here, because it's like, you know, almost 12.30 now. So she says to me, no, no, they're already rented. I say, well, how about this? I said, I have no idea. Did they pay for the jet skis? And she goes, no, they haven't. I said, well, how about this? They didn't pay for the jet skis. Why don't you just let me pay for the whole hour? They're sitting right there, and I'll keep, me and my kids will stay right here where you can see us. We'll just do some circles around. And this way, if they come back, you can just give them the jet skis, and I'll pay for the whole hour anyway. And she goes, I'm sorry, they're rented. I'm like, it's the rules. I'm like, oh no, whack, whack, duck, right? Rules, the rules. All year from duck peoples, it's in possibility notion. So I say to her, what you've probably said to other people in the situation, I said, let me speak to somebody else. And she replies, I'm sorry, sir, they're going to tell you the same thing as me. I said, no, no, find me somebody else who doesn't think it can't be done. So just find me something that believes it's a possibility. And she's like, fine. And she's like, hold on. And she starts walking behind these double doors. And I say, stop. Don't move. And she's like, what? I said, don't go behind those double doors. And she says, why not? I said, duck pond back there. More ducks. I said, go upstairs and find me an eagle. She looked at me and I was crazy. Anyway, the hotel manager came down from upstairs and sure enough, boom, I got my jet skis in two minutes flat because she was an eagle. And this is how the inner game melds with the outer game. See if you see yourself going into that duck-like mentality ever, especially if you're in front of a prospect, you've got to break out of it. You know, understand this. I know there are no ducks in this training room. You're not a duck. I get that. But what happens is very often as eagles through the trials and tribulations of lives, we get our wings clipped. Some bad stuff happens, some mistakes either our own fault sometimes or no fault of our own. Some things happen, bam, you get your wings clipped and you start developing this impossibility notion. You start focusing on the reasons of why you can't get ahead in life, you can't get what you want and your wings are clipped and all of a sudden you start acting like a duck. You're still an eagle. The bottom line is this. From this module, your path starts here to being the highest, the best eagle you can possibly be. Your brain is constantly weighing these options and choosing a path to every second of day. And if you're not moving towards your goal, it's because your standards are not aligned with your actual vision and it's creating some disconnect. You've got to get out of your comfort zone to really succeed in life and to take your sales, your income and all the errors of your life that are important to you to take them to the next level. You can't let yourself be comfortable with where you are right now and settle for a life that's anything less than your fully capable of living. And I put this module in this program because I can teach you all the straight line strategies in the world. I can give you every nuance of how to close the sale and take someone down the straight line and literally close anyone who's closeable. But if you don't have the inner game stuff, those inner game elements lined up, if you don't have your standards at the highest level to match your vision at that determination, you're wide to see things through, you're simply not going to follow through for the long term.
